text: For a matter of clearness, the first steps of this procedure will not be shown in this video.
We begin here with the fine bronchus dissections thanks to the Maryland and the fenestrated
The surgical challenge of this moment is to go around the bronchus within its sticky environment due to the history of radiotherapy.
Notice how this fine bronchus dissection is possible thanks to the accuracy and the large range of motion of the robotic tools.
With a bit of patience and a careful dissection, the surgeon managed to safely individualize the bronchus.
After the stapling of the bronchus, the dissection of the mediastinal lymph node begins.
For the record, this 3 cm lymph node is located in the lower right paratracheal station.
We can see here that the robotic tools allow the surgeon to turn around the lymph node structure with respect of its environment.
The lymph node is now adequately dissected.
The pulmonary artery is free and can be stapled safely.
The remaining steps consist in the section of the pulmonary parenchyma concluding the resection of the nodule and the suspicious lymph node in one piece.
This video demonstrates the usefulness of the robotic assisted thoracic surgery for the resection of a nodule associated with a big lymph node.
